findings.title: Test file cleanup removes unused imports
findings.description: Removed 6 unused imports (json, subprocess, sys, tempfile, os, pathlib) from test file

analysis.before_fix: Imports included json, subprocess, sys, tempfile, os, pathlib but none were used in tests
analysis.after_fix: Only unittest.mock and pytest imports remain, both are used in tests

impact.code_clarity: Improved - test file now has only necessary imports
impact.maintenance: Easier to maintain with fewer unused imports
impact.test_behavior: No change - tests still function identically

findings.why_it_matters: Unused imports in test files can mask actual import errors and increase cognitive load
findings.notes: Good practice - test file cleaned up properly

findings.title: Test file cleanup removes unused import
findings.description: Removed unused pathlib import from test file

analysis.before_fix: from pathlib import Path was present but Path not used in tests
analysis.after_fix: Import removed, test still uses unittest.mock and pytest

findings.why_it_matters: Unused imports in tests are often left after refactoring and should be cleaned up
findings.notes: Good practice - minor cleanup improves code clarity

findings.title: Test file coverage gaps in test_analysis_parser.py
findings.description: Test_extract_project_metadata_pyproject_toml_with_scripts is marked as pass with no actual test

issue.reason: The test_project fixture doesn't have scripts in pyproject.toml

recommendations: Add pyproject.toml with scripts section to test_project fixture
Or remove the test entirely if not needed
Or mark it as skip with explanation
